title: Town For Sale

jokes.text: Told you I'd sell this town. Didn't I tell you? / Yes. Not to diminish your accomplishment, but you did say that a handful of times. / I think eight, to be exact.
jokes.setup: Johnny bragging about selling the town
jokes.punchline: David's precise count of how many times he's said it
jokes.explanation: Classic David dry wit with his specific count undermining Johnny's moment

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I've told this story forty-seven times.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Forty-eight.

jokes.text: My little suity-cases! Oh!
jokes.setup: Normal person would say suitcases
jokes.punchline: Moira's affected pronunciation combining 'suit' and 'suitcases'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira-ism that combines her pretentiousness with made-up vocabulary

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Suitcatches.

jokes.text: If I'm being perfectly honest, it's very low on the totem pole of coats of yours that I like.
jokes.setup: Moira asking if she should take a coat
jokes.punchline: David's brutally honest assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect David honesty delivered with his characteristic passive-aggressive politeness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You should take a coat. You look like you're about to commit a crime, and I'd prefer the police have as little to go on as possible.

jokes.text: I outbid Richard Branson for it at the Kaminski auction.
jokes.setup: Defending a coat David doesn't like
jokes.punchline: Absurd name-drop about outbidding a billionaire
jokes.explanation: Shows Johnny's past wealth through absurd casual reference

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: This coat is a Valentino. Do you know how exclusive this is? I outbid Elon Musk for this coat at auction.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, you did not outbid Elon Musk for a coat.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, he wasn't there. But I would have.

jokes.text: It would be her first piece of clothing made outside of mainland China.
jokes.setup: Talking about giving coat to Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: Moira's snobbish assumption about Jocelyn's wardrobe
jokes.explanation: Shows Moira's classist assumptions delivered with her characteristic superiority

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm giving my coat to Jocelyn. She needs it far more than I do.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's very generous of you.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, the poor woman has been wearing the same three cardigans since we met her. I doubt she even owns a proper coat.

jokes.text: I thought everyone just kind of dropped you like flies when you couldn't pick up the tab at the Waverly anymore?
jokes.setup: David claiming he has friends in New York
jokes.punchline: Alexis brutally pointing out his fair-weather friends
jokes.explanation: Alexis cuts through David's denial with painful accuracy

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, those aren't your friends. Those are just people who knew you when you had money.

jokes.text: I just have a different kind of relationship with my friends where we don't need to be in constant contact with each other.
jokes.setup: Alexis calling out his fake friends
jokes.punchline: David's defensive rationalization of being abandoned
jokes.explanation: David's elaborate excuse for having no real friends shows his self-deception

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, these people aren't your friends. They abandoned you the moment things got difficult.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not fair. They didn't abandon me. They simply chose not to maintain contact during a transitional period in my life.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Which, when you think about it, is a very reasonable response to someone losing their entire fortune and moving to a small town.

jokes.text: And do what, eat a cronut at a Brooklyn flea market?
jokes.setup: David suggesting she come to New York
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dismissive view of trendy Brooklyn activities
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's superficial understanding of NYC through hipster stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, I'm not moving to Brooklyn to sit in a coffee shop and watch someone make a pour-over while I pretend to care about their sustainable sourcing practices.

jokes.text: so I can pack how I wanna pack and I don't have to be called a circus freak all the time.
jokes.setup: Alexis wanting her own place
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to David calling her a circus freak
jokes.explanation: Shows the siblings' toxic dynamic through Alexis's casual mention of abuse

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just think it's time for me to get my own place, you know? I need my independence.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, you can barely take care of yourself. You're like a circus freak.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, David, that is so mean.

jokes.text: Is it a wig?
jokes.setup: Moira giving Jocelyn a coat
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn can't identify what the coat is
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - Jocelyn's confusion about basic clothing shows the culture gap

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This is a vintage Halston. I wore it to a benefit in the '80s.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: It's beautiful. What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's a coat, Jocelyn.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: No, I mean... what animal is it?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Ostrich.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh! Because I was going to say it looked like someone plucked a very large, very angry bird.

jokes.text: The coat you kept going on about that one time. / Are you sure it was me?
jokes.setup: Moira claiming Jocelyn coveted the coat
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn has no memory of this
jokes.explanation: Shows Moira's self-delusion about others wanting her things

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You were absolutely green with envy over this coat.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I have no memory of that.

jokes.text: Oh, it's absolutely tragic, Jocelyn, that I will never see or speak to you ever again.
jokes.setup: Giving Jocelyn a goodbye gift
jokes.punchline: Moira's melodramatic fake emotion about their 'friendship'
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical insincerity perfectly captures her character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Jocelyn, darling, this gift is a token of the profound bond we've shared. Our friendship has been... *pauses dramatically* ...transformative. You've been like a sister to me. A sister I barely tolerated, but a sister nonetheless.

jokes.text: What's an all-inclusive vacation?
jokes.setup: Ted mentioning all-inclusive vacation
jokes.punchline: Alexis doesn't know what that means despite being worldly
jokes.explanation: Reveals Alexis's sheltered wealthy upbringing through what she doesn't know

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: You should really consider an all-inclusive vacation. You know, where everything is included?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what do you mean 'everything'? Like, the flights?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yes, the flights, the hotel, the meals, the drinks, all the activities...

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that sounds exhausting. Why would I want everything included? I like choosing things.

jokes.text: If I was staying, then yes! A thousand times yes! And if things were different and I wasn't leaving, um, then definitely yes. / So, is that a no? / Yes.
jokes.setup: Ted's marriage proposal
jokes.punchline: Alexis's confusing conditional acceptance that's actually a rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the painful confusion of the moment through Alexis's contradictory response

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, will you marry me?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes! I mean... if you were someone else, then yes.

jokes.text: Oh, you just watch a season of Girls and do the opposite of what they do. It's easy.
jokes.setup: Stevie saying she doesn't know what to do in New York
jokes.punchline: David's dismissive pop culture reference as life advice
jokes.explanation: Shows David's superficial understanding masked as wisdom

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Just do what Carrie Bradshaw would do.

jokes.text: I mean, I was looking at three bedrooms, but do I really need an office?
jokes.setup: Describing apartment options
jokes.punchline: David pretending he has work that requires an office
jokes.explanation: Shows David's delusions about his professional importance

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I actually need an office. I have a very demanding job that requires a separate workspace.

jokes.text: Yeah, judging by your tips, I was never really sure.
jokes.setup: Johnny thanking Twyla for great service
jokes.punchline: Twyla's dry observation about his poor tipping
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery undermines Johnny's self-perception

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Thank you so much for the excellent service, Twyla. You've been wonderful.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, thank you, Mr. Rose. Though I have to say, your tip is about as generous as your compliments are frequent.

jokes.text: I don't wanna piss in your punch bowl, okay, but, um, I got a bad feeling about this buyer guy
jokes.setup: Roland warning Johnny about the buyer
jokes.punchline: Roland's crude metaphor for delivering bad news
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland crude expression delivered with his characteristic lack of tact

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yeah, look, I gotta tell you something, and I'm not gonna sugarcoat it. It's like when you got a dead cat in your pool — you can't just leave it there hoping it'll float away. Eventually somebody's gotta jump in and fish it out.

jokes.text: He called me while he was bumping uglies with his old lady. / But that's not the problem. The problem is he put me on the speakerphone.
jokes.setup: Roland complaining about the buyer
jokes.punchline: The speakerphone during sex is worse than the sex call itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - the speakerphone violation is worse than the sexual overshare

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The speakerphone during sex is worse than the sex call itself

jokes.text: I mean, who would do such a thing? / I mean, that's something you would do. / No, no. That's not something I would do. / And I can't deal with another you.
jokes.setup: Roland complaining about inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately compares it to Johnny, then insults him
jokes.explanation: Shows Roland's complete lack of filter and Johnny's wounded dignity

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: That's inappropriate behavior. It's like when Johnny does that thing where he just stands there looking all confused and useless.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm right here, Roland.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Exactly my point.

jokes.text: The douchebag is right here.
jokes.setup: Buyer asking for Johnny Rose, calling him a douchebag
jokes.punchline: Johnny identifying himself while hearing the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - Johnny's dignity gets immediately crushed

dialogue.speaker: Buyer
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm looking for a douchebag named Johnny Rose.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, you've found him.

jokes.text: I'm not a dolphin, Mutt.
jokes.setup: Mutt saying she's going back to her natural habitat
jokes.punchline: Alexis's literal interpretation and animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist response that shows Alexis's unique perspective

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I'm going back to my natural habitat.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, are you like, a penguin or something?

jokes.text: You should really work on expanding your emotional range at some point.
jokes.setup: Mutt's typically understated reaction to her leaving
jokes.punchline: Alexis's direct critique of his emotional limitations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Alexis calling out Mutt's emotional detachment with typical directness.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know, it's actually really attractive how you can just... let me go like that. Most guys would at least pretend to care.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I do care.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, you have a really weird way of showing it.

jokes.text: So, what you're saying is he got a little short with you? / You, uh, weren't quite seeing eye to eye?
jokes.setup: Roland describing conflict with short buyer
jokes.punchline: Johnny's height-based puns about their disagreement
jokes.explanation: Predictable height puns that feel forced rather than clever

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, it sounds like you two really didn't see eye to eye.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I suppose the deal fell through because he was looking for something a bit more... beneath you?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Or perhaps he simply didn't measure up to your expectations.

jokes.text: Knockoffs are getting so good these days.
jokes.setup: Twyla wearing designer coat Jocelyn gave her
jokes.punchline: Twyla assumes it's fake because Jocelyn couldn't have real designer
jokes.explanation: Good irony - genuine designer coat assumed to be fake due to source

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: This is a real designer coat? Jocelyn gave me this? There's no way this is real.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: It is real, Twyla.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: No, it can't be. You don't have that kind of money.

jokes.text: I'm gonna feel like Patty LaBelle.
jokes.setup: Twyla excited about wearing the coat in winter
jokes.punchline: Her specific reference point for glamour
jokes.explanation: Perfect Twyla - her reference points for glamour are charmingly different from the Roses' world.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I'm so excited to wear this coat in winter. It's so glamorous.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: It reminds me of the coats the women wear at the bingo hall.

jokes.text: I'm gonna feel like Patty LaBelle.
jokes.setup: Twyla excited about wearing the coat in winter
jokes.punchline: Her specific reference to feeling glamorous like Patty LaBelle
jokes.explanation: Shows Twyla's humble excitement through specific celebrity comparison

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I feel so glamorous in this coat. I feel like Patty LaBelle.

jokes.text: His hours were up a month ago, he kept coming anyway. / What? / Yeah, maybe or maybe he just likes pretty girls in hippie hats.
jokes.setup: Ray explaining Mutt's work performance
jokes.punchline: Revealing Mutt stayed to be near Alexis, with awkward flirtation
jokes.explanation: Reveals romantic subplot while showing Ray's inappropriate workplace comments

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Well, Mutt's been doing exemplary work. Really top-notch performance.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Yes, well, I think it has more to do with the fact that you've been working here.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I just... I like being around you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

jokes.text: I like you. I don't want to like you, but I do.
jokes.setup: David asking why Stevie won't come to New York
jokes.punchline: Stevie's brutally honest confession of unwanted feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie moment - emotionally honest while maintaining her defensive humor

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm not going to New York because I don't want to watch you fall in love with someone else.

jokes.text: You've been my only friend, so... Great, nevertheless.
jokes.setup: David saying Stevie's been a great friend
jokes.punchline: Admitting she's his only friend, then trying to recover
jokes.explanation: David's vulnerability accidentally revealed, then awkwardly covered

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Stevie's been a great friend.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: My only friend, really.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I mean, not my only friend. I have other friends. Plenty of them. All over the place.

jokes.text: Yeah, not as many as you'd think, so...
jokes.setup: Stevie says he has lots of friends who'd want to live with him
jokes.punchline: David's honest admission about his social isolation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier friend discussion. David's understated honesty about his loneliness is touching and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You have lots of friends who'd want to live with you?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I have you. And I consider you a friend. So that's one. And then there's my mother, who I'm forced to see regularly. So that's two people who have to tolerate me because of legal or emotional obligation. And then there's Patrick, but he works for me, so I'm not sure that counts. So really, I have no friends.

jokes.text: That is the nicest thing anyone has ever said to me.
jokes.setup: David calling their time together 'survivable'
jokes.punchline: Stevie's sarcastic appreciation of the backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan response to David's typically tactless comment

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh wow, 'survivable.' That's so romantic. I'm going to have that embroidered on a pillow.

jokes.text: Sex in a pan. / Then we'll have dessert.
jokes.setup: Roland describing dessert as 'sex in a pan'
jokes.punchline: Andy's sexual innuendo response
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual innuendo but fits the crude characters

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It's like sex in a pan.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not sure I'd know what that tastes like.

jokes.text: You didn't try it on, did you? / No. / Oh, why not? / I gave it away.
jokes.setup: Moira asking about the coat she gifted
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn casually admitting she re-gifted it immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Jocelyn's obliviousness to social rules

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Did you ever wear that coat I gave you?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, well, I gave it to my sister right away.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You re-gifted my coat?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: It wasn't really my style.

jokes.text: Smoking in a car with a baby, unless you crack a window, tipping before tax, mixing drinks with cola, and giving away a coat that doesn't belong to you.
jokes.setup: Moira listing things that are 'just not done'
jokes.punchline: Escalating from harmful to petty with equal moral weight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Moira's warped priorities - baby endangerment equals coat etiquette

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: One simply does not wear white after Labor Day, one does not return a gift, and one does not use the word 'moist' in polite company. It's just not done.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And frankly, neither is microwaving fish in a shared office space, or asking someone how much they paid for their home, or wearing Crocs to the theatre.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Absolutely forbidden. Simply barbaric.

jokes.text: Why did you do all that extra community service, Mutt? / I like to stay active. / Really?
jokes.setup: Alexis pressing Mutt about his motivations
jokes.punchline: Mutt's obvious lie about staying active
jokes.explanation: Shows Mutt's emotional unavailability through his transparent deflection

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So why are you really staying in Schitt's Creek?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I just want to stay active.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You literally sit in a barn all day.

jokes.text: You know why! / Why can't you just say it?
jokes.setup: Alexis demanding Mutt explain his extra community service
jokes.punchline: The emotional confrontation ends with him unable to express feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Mutt's emotional constipation vs Alexis's need for verbal validation. Cringe-funny.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mutt, you need to explain to me right now why you have extra community service.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Alexis, I... it's complicated.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Complicated? We're supposed to be together and you can't even talk to me about this?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I know, and I'm sorry. I just... I don't know how to say this.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, you're going to have to figure it out because I deserve an explanation.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: You're right. You do. I just... I can't seem to find the words.

jokes.text: Talk to the hand, son, because I've gotta watch a pig eat.
jokes.setup: David demanding money from the town sale
jokes.punchline: Johnny dismisses him with dated slang and bizarre priority
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - 'talk to the hand' is perfect Johnny, and 'watch a pig eat' is wonderfully bizarre.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, I need to talk to you about the money from the town sale.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, not now. I'm trying to figure out if this fanny pack is coming back in style.

jokes.text: Is it just me, or is it this dessert that's making me horny?
jokes.setup: Eating dinner with potential business partners
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate sexual comment at dinner table
jokes.explanation: Establishes Andy as inappropriate but predictable crude humor

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I was just thinking, you know, with the right attitude and a little bit of flexibility, we could really make this thing work between us.

jokes.text: $975,000 suddenly seems like such a girly number. / I mean a million. That's bold, that's sexy.
jokes.setup: Contract ready to be signed
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's bizarre gendering of numbers and sales tactics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment showing Jocelyn's unique 'business' logic

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Okay, so I have the contract ready for you to sign. Now, the numbers look really good. We've got some beautiful female numbers here, and then some strong, masculine numbers over here.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: But honestly, I'm more of a numbers person than a sales person, so I'm just going to let the numbers do the talking.

jokes.text: Andy collapses and can't sign the contract
jokes.setup: About to sign the million-dollar contract
jokes.punchline: Buyer collapses at the crucial moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - ultimate frustration at the worst possible moment

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, the buyer just collapsed. I think he's having a heart attack.

jokes.text: Roland and Johnny frantically trying to get Andy to sign while unconscious
jokes.setup: Andy has collapsed
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempts to complete the sale on an unconscious man
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing how desperate they are to escape the town

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Is he okay? Should we call an ambulance?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Ambulance? Are you crazy? We're in the middle of closing a deal here. Andy, buddy, just sign right here. Your hand can do it. Come on.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, the man is unconscious.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: An unconscious signature is still a signature, John. It's legally binding. I looked it up.

jokes.text: People do come out of comas. / Oh, I'd kill for a good coma right now.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to stay optimistic about the buyer
jokes.punchline: Moira's dark wish for unconsciousness to escape reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Moira humor - wishing for coma as vacation from disappointment

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, the buyer seemed very interested. I think this could actually work out.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, well, I'm going to go home, take some pills, and hope I slip into a coma.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure I'm engaged, and I'm pretty sure I just cheated on my fiance.
jokes.setup: Family asking if Alexis will be okay
jokes.punchline: Alexis's confused relationship status after the day's events
jokes.explanation: Captures Alexis's confusion about her romantic obligations with typical uncertainty

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, I'm not sure what my relationship status is at this point. Like, am I dating him? Are we just... existing in the same space? I honestly don't know.

tmdb_overview: Johnny finds a potential buyer for the town, while David and Alexis discover they may have become more attached to Schitt’s Creek than they ever thought possible.
insight: Season finale lands 82 with character-driven cringe as Rose family hits rock bottom together.
